req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
preconditions: source.owner == target.owner

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-028 specifies a $25,000/day transfer cap between own accounts with atomic execution. The partition space splits into: valid transfers (0 < amount <= 25000), over-limit transfers (amount > 25000), and exact boundary points (amount == 25000, amount == 25001). Additional negative paths cover insufficient funds (409) and cross-owner transfers (precondition violation). BVA drives the boundary suite at $25,000 (on-point, must pass) and $25,001 (off-point, must be declined with 422). EP covers a mid-range positive case ($1,000) and an over-limit negative case ($30,000). DT covers the combined condition of daily-limit-exceeded AND insufficient-funds ordering. STC models account balance state transitions across a successful transfer sequence to confirm atomicity. The violation in the original response was test[5] having kind=negative with heuristic=STC; STC (State Transition Coverage) is a structural/positive technique and must be paired with kind=positive or kind=boundary. That test has been corrected to kind=positive with heuristic=STC, modelling the valid state transition from funded->transferred state.

tests.name: test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $1,000
No prior transfers made today (daily total = $0)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=1000, valid source_account_id, valid target_account_id
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds(api_client, own_accounts_fixture, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_fixture
    src_balance_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_balance_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json={
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": 1000
    })

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body

    src_balance_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_balance_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_balance_after == src_balance_before - 1000
    assert tgt_balance_after == tgt_balance_before + 1000

tests.name: test_transfer_exceeds_daily_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $30,000
No prior transfers made today (daily total = $0)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=30000, valid source_account_id, valid target_account_id
trace: R-FIN-028: error pathway 422 when daily limit exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_exceeds_daily_limit_returns_422(api_client, own_accounts_fixture, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_fixture
    src_balance_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_balance_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json={
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": 30000
    })

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert resp.json().get("error_code") == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"

    src_balance_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_balance_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_balance_after == src_balance_before
    assert tgt_balance_after == tgt_balance_before

tests.name: test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_on_point_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $25,000
No prior transfers made today (daily total = $0)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=25000, valid source_account_id, valid target_account_id
trace: R-FIN-028: BVA on-point at maximum allowed daily transfer ($25,000)
R-FIN-028: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must pass
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_on_point_succeeds(api_client, own_accounts_fixture, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_fixture
    src_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json={
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": 25000
    })

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    assert "transfer_id" in resp.json()

    src_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_after == src_before - 25000
    assert tgt_after == tgt_before + 25000

tests.name: test_transfer_one_above_daily_limit_off_point_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $25,001
No prior transfers made today (daily total = $0)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=25001, valid source_account_id, valid target_account_id
trace: R-FIN-028: BVA off-point one cent above maximum ($25,001)
R-FIN-028: boundary behaviour at exact max+1 threshold must be declined
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_one_above_daily_limit_off_point_returns_422(api_client, own_accounts_fixture, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_fixture
    src_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json={
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": 25001
    })

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert resp.json().get("error_code") == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"

    src_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_after == src_before
    assert tgt_after == tgt_before

tests.name: test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance is $100
No prior transfers made today (daily total = $0)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=500, valid source_account_id, valid target_account_id
trace: R-FIN-028: error pathway 409 on insufficient funds
R-FIN-028: funds moved atomically implies rollback on failure
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, own_accounts_low_balance_fixture, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_low_balance_fixture  # src balance seeded to $100
    src_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json={
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": 500
    })

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    assert resp.json().get("error_code") == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"

    src_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_after == src_before
    assert tgt_after == tgt_before

tests.name: test_transfer_atomicity_state_transition_funded_to_transferred
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $5,000
No prior transfers made today (daily total = $0)
System is in state: READY (no pending transfers)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=5000, valid source_account_id, valid target_account_id
GET /accounts/{source_account_id} and GET /accounts/{target_account_id} to confirm final balances
expected: Transfer transitions through states: PENDING -> COMPLETED (no intermediate FAILED state observed)
Source account balance decreases by exactly $5,000 after COMPLETED state
Target account balance increases by exactly $5,000 after COMPLETED state
Daily transfer total for customer increases by $5,000
No partial balance update visible at any observable state
trace: R-FIN-028: STC state transition READY->PENDING->COMPLETED for valid intra-customer transfer
R-FIN-028: funds moved atomically - both debit and credit must reflect in COMPLETED state
R-FIN-028: precondition source.owner == target.owner satisfied throughout transition
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_atomicity_state_transition_funded_to_transferred(api_client, own_accounts_fixture, reset_daily_limit):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_fixture
    src_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json={
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": 5000
    })

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    transfer_id = body["transfer_id"]

    # Poll transfer state to confirm COMPLETED (no FAILED state)
    import time
    for _ in range(10):
        transfer_resp = api_client.get(f"/transfers/{transfer_id}")
        assert transfer_resp.status_code == 200
        state = transfer_resp.json().get("status")
        assert state != "FAILED", "Transfer must not enter FAILED state on valid funded transfer"
        if state == "COMPLETED":
            break
        time.sleep(0.5)
    else:
        raise AssertionError("Transfer did not reach COMPLETED state within timeout")

    src_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_after == src_before - 5000, "Source balance must decrease atomically by transfer amount"
    assert tgt_after == tgt_before + 5000, "Target balance must increase atomically by transfer amount"

tests.name: test_transfer_daily_limit_accumulation_second_transfer_exceeds_cap
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
Source account balance >= $25,001
First transfer of $20,000 already completed today (daily total = $20,000)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=5001, valid source_account_id, valid target_account_id (cumulative total would be $25,001)
trace: R-FIN-028: DT condition combination - individual amount within limit BUT cumulative daily total exceeds $25,000
R-FIN-028: error pathway 422 when daily limit exceeded (via accumulation, not single transfer)
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_daily_limit_accumulation_second_transfer_exceeds_cap(api_client, own_accounts_fixture, seed_daily_transfer_total):
    src, tgt = own_accounts_fixture
    # Seed daily total to $20,000 via fixture
    seed_daily_transfer_total(src, 20000)

    src_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json={
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": 5001
    })

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert resp.json().get("error_code") == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"

    src_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src}").json()["balance"]
    tgt_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt}").json()["balance"]
    assert src_after == src_before
    assert tgt_after == tgt_before
